seo.title: LangGraph Guardrails for Fintech
seo.description: Compliance guide for LangGraph agents in fintech. Meet SOX, PCI DSS requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Fintech teams using LangGraph face heightened compliance risks due to the framework's complex, cyclical agent workflows that can lead to infinite loops, state corruption, or bypassed human oversight. These failure modes amplify vulnerabilities in handling sensitive financial data, transaction processing, and regulatory decision-making under SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR, and SEC rules. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify state integrity, enforce compliance checkpoints, and prevent catastrophic errors that could result in financial losses or regulatory penalties.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires accurate financial reporting and internal controls over stateful processes to prevent material misstatements from graph execution errors.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies state transitions and checkpoint integrity, ensuring no cyclic executions or deserialization errors corrupt financial records.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates encryption of cardholder data at rest and in transit, with audit trails for all data flows in agent graphs.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces encryption policies on graph state and logs all parallel branch merges to maintain PCI-compliant data protection.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires data minimization, consent tracking, and right-to-erasure in customer data processing workflows.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates PII handling in graph nodes and prevents unauthorized state persistence across cycles.

complianceRequirements.description: Financial advice must include clear disclaimers and explainable recommendations from agent decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex mandates disclaimer injection and explainability checks before graph outputs reach customers.

industryRisks.name: Cyclic Approval Loop
industryRisks.description: Infinite graph cycles bypass human-in-the-loop for high-value transactions, violating BSA/AML controls.
industryRisks.example: AML review node loops indefinitely on $10M wire transfer, delaying detection of suspicious activity.

industryRisks.name: Parallel Credit Scoring Conflict
industryRisks.description: Merging conflicting scores from model branches corrupts credit decisions without explainability.
industryRisks.example: One branch approves loan while another flags fraud, resulting in unexplainable $500K credit extension.

industryRisks.name: Checkpoint Schema Drift
industryRisks.description: Deserialization failures after model updates expose unencrypted transaction data.
industryRisks.example: PCI tokenization schema change crashes payment graph, leaking 10K card numbers in memory.

industryRisks.name: Bypassed Compliance Node
industryRisks.description: Conditional edges skip SOX disclosure checks during high-frequency trading workflows.
industryRisks.example: Trade recommendation graph routes around disclaimer node, violating SEC Reg BI.

implementation.title: Wrap graph.invoke() with Vex Verification
implementation.description: Protect against cyclic execution and state corruption by verifying inputs/outputs around LangGraph invocation.
implementation.code:
import vex
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph

# Fintech transaction approval graph
graph = StateGraph(TransactionState)
# ... add nodes and edges ...
app = graph.compile()

@vex.verify(policies=["no-infinite-loops", "state-schema-v2", "pci-encryption"])
def safe_invoke(input_state):
    result = app.invoke(input_state)
    return result

# Usage
result = safe_invoke({
    'amount': 100000,
    'customer_id': 'CUST123',
    'aml_score': 0.7
})

implementation.title: Vex as LangGraph Compliance Node
implementation.description: Add Vex as a dedicated node for real-time compliance checks before state merges and human handoffs.
implementation.code:
import vex
from langgraph.graph import END
from typing import TypedDict

class TransactionState(TypedDict):
    amount: float
    aml_cleared: bool
    disclaimer_added: bool

@vex.node(policies=["sox-controls", "sec-bi-disclosure", "gdpr-pii-scan"])
def compliance_check(state: TransactionState) -> TransactionState:
    if state['amount'] > 50000 and not state['aml_cleared']:
        raise vex.Violation("High-value transaction requires AML clearance")
    state['disclaimer_added'] = True
    return state

# In graph builder
graph.add_node("compliance", compliance_check)
graph.add_edge("aml_review", "compliance")
graph.add_conditional_edges("compliance", lambda s: "human_review" if s['amount'] > 100000 else END)

implementation.title: State Corruption Guard with Vex
implementation.description: Prevent parallel branch merge conflicts corrupting financial state using Vex schema validation.

caseStudyScenario.heading: LangGraph in Fintech: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized neobank deployed LangGraph to orchestrate their loan origination workflow, combining AML screening, credit scoring from three models, regulatory disclosure generation, and human underwriter approval in a cyclical graph. The system processed 5,000 applications daily across parallel branches that merged risk scores and compliance flags. Without proper guardrails, the deployment suffered from state corruption when FICO and internal model branches produced conflicting scores, cyclic loops during peak hours that bypassed human review for $2M+ loans, and checkpoint failures after a schema update leaked unencrypted SSN data in memory.

These issues triggered an SEC investigation after 127 loans were approved without Reg BI disclaimers, exposing the bank to $8.7M in potential fines and customer lawsuits. Regulators flagged the opaque decision-making process and lack of auditability in the agentic workflow.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex guardrails, the neobank achieved 100% compliance across 2.3M transactions. Vex prevented 847 cyclic execution attempts, enforced schema validation on all checkpoint saves, and automatically injected SEC-compliant disclaimers. The parallel branch merger now validates score reconciliation with explainability reports, reducing credit decision errors by 97%. Regulators cleared the audit with zero findings, and the bank scaled to 15,000 daily applications while maintaining SOX control integrity.

cta.heading: Comply with Fintech Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex guardrails into your LangGraph workflows today to eliminate compliance risks and scale confidently.
